Discovery: Q1 2026 total revenue +87% YoY to $1.28B driven by AI chip test demand surge; robotics division growing sequentially each quarter ($91M Q1 2026 vs $69M Q1 2025) as collaborative robot deployments scale with manufacturing reshoring — dual tailwind from chip test boom AND physical AI.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Analyst targets $430-440 vs $363 = real upside; Q2 guide sequential step-down spooked market -18%; 70% AI-driven revenue but cyclical equipment

catalysts_general: 51.5% drawdown from $109.50 ATH; EP electrophysiology demand miss + securities fraud class action overhang; core neuromodulation +15%; scale into 1/3 position only (Tier 4 — FierceBiotech class action coverage)
growth_picks.analyst_take: Down 51.5% from $109.50 high; core business intact (neuromodulation +15%); management credibility overhang from EP miss and securities fraud class action

catalysts_general: Kazatomprom cut 2026 output ~10% from 32,777 to 29,697 MT U3O8; spot uranium peaked $101/lb; Chinese strategic buying diverting supply; 5 Buy analysts avg $136-139 (Tier 1 — INN; Cameco IR)

Discovery: Kazatomprom (world's largest uranium producer, ~40% of global supply) cut 2026 output 10% and faces disrupted export routes through Russia due to sanctions, while Chinese strategic buying is redirecting remaining supply away from Western utilities; Cameco as the premier Western-allied producer is the default beneficiary with spot uranium peaking $101/lb in 2026.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Dominant Western uranium producer; Kazatomprom cut 10% output; AI data center nuclear renaissance; but 59.5x Fwd P/E — priced fairly, wait for dip below $110

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: 50% drawdown driven by elegrobart (36% vs 51% Tepezza proptosis responders); Amgen's Tepezza SC formulation gaining late-stage data threatening veligrotug launch position; $762M cash provides runway (Tier 4 — BioPharma Dive; FierceBiotech)

Discovery: PDUFA June 30, 2026 for veligrotug in thyroid eye disease (TED) — Breakthrough Therapy + Priority Review, positive Phase 3 data from THRIVE, THRIVE-2, REVEAL-1, and REVEAL-2 trials all hitting primary endpoints; $762M cash and launch-ready commercial team in place.
growth_picks.analyst_take: June 30 PDUFA for veligrotug valid but Tepezza SC (Amgen subcutaneous) is gaining data that threatens post-launch market share; elegrobart disappointment drove the 50% drawdown

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: CEO Elliott Hill bought 23,660 shares at $42.27 April 13; Tim Cook (board) bought 25,000 shares April 10; Q4 guide -2% to -4% revenue, China -20%; EPS estimate cut from $2.16 to $1.58 (Tier 1 — CNBC earnings; Form 4)

Discovery: Down 47.3% from 52-week high ($80.17 to $42.27) — lowest print since 2014 — with CEO Elliott Hill purchasing $1M of stock personally in April 2026 (Form 4 confirmed insider buy at the lows), while North America is showing real recovery signals (revenue +3%, wholesale +11%, Running category +20%+) and Q4 FY2026 earnings due late June/July provide the first clean catalyst to prove the turnaround is printing in the numbers.
growth_picks.analyst_take: CEO Hill + Tim Cook dual insider buy at 12-year price lows; but China -20% guided Q4 and real Fwd P/E closer to 27x on $1.58 EPS — wait for Q4 confirmation

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: DATA INTEGRITY FLAG: COO International sold 7,000, COO Benefits sold 5,776, COO Carriedo sold 1,575 shares; only one director bought 1,000 shares; discovery claim of broad cluster buy is directly contradicted (Tier 1 — Quiverquant Form 4)

Discovery: Unusually broad insider cluster — Chair, CFO, multiple COOs, and technology/legal heads all bought stock within a tight window, with stock down ~24% over 3 years at ~$149; fleet payments and digital SaaS businesses positioned for recovery.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Discovery insider cluster buy signal is WRONG — Form 4 shows net heavy insider SELLING by multiple COOs; no entry without signal verification

catalysts_general: Q1 FY2026: revenue $151M (+51%), gross margin 42%; FY2026 guide raised to $720-735M revenue but below Street consensus $740M; -15.4% selloff; $1B+ funded backlog (Tier 1 — Benzinga)

Discovery: Pure-play defense/space manufacturer posting 51% revenue growth Q1 2026 driven by drone, missile, and hypersonics demand — down 26% from highs with analyst consensus Buy and 81% upside to $106 target; expanding Utah missile facility quadruples loitering munition output.
growth_picks.analyst_take: 51% revenue growth, 42% margins, $1B+ backlog in hypersonics; but P/E 292x, FCF negative, and market -15% on raised-but-missed-consensus guidance is a warning

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Net new ARR $265M (+73% YoY); June 3 earnings; analyst consensus $500 is 11% below current $562; Fwd P/E 91.2 leaves zero margin for error at current price (Tier 1 — CrowdStrike IR; Tier 3 — multiple analysts)

Discovery: Iran conflict has unleashed state-sponsored cyber attacks documented in CrowdStrike's 2026 Global Threat Report, with Iran-linked hackers explicitly cited by CISA in April 2026; government defense budgets expanding for cyber capabilities, and CrowdStrike's acquisition of Seraphic Security (Jan 2026) positions it for an M&A-driven consolidation wave in defense cybersecurity.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Analyst consensus $500 is 11% BELOW current $562; Fwd P/E 91.2 PEG 4.5 — wrong price for a good company; wait for pullback to $490-520

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Q1 2026: revenue $4.6B (+59%), EBITDA guide raised to $8.2-8.5B (4x increase); Citi upgraded Buy PT $17; MS Overweight $22; float-adjusted 94% short interest mechanically possible squeeze (Tier 3 — Citi; MS upgrades)

Discovery: LNG exporter upgraded to Buy ($17-19 target) after 70% decline from IPO; LNG outage tightening global spot supply to $15/BTU could boost 2026 EBITDA to $9B and unlock dividends — institutional tailwind as energy sector leads 2026 rotation.
growth_picks.analyst_take: 4x EBITDA guidance raise; Citi Buy $17; captures LNG spot upside but FCF -$10.85B and D/E 293 — tactical only, LNG (Cheniere) is superior

catalysts_general: Q1 2026: revenue $639.8M (+34%), FCF $84.1M; company cut 20% of workforce (1,100) citing AI replacing functions; stock -24% post-beat — market interprets layoffs as moat erosion signal (Tier 1 — CNBC)

Discovery: Stock -24% post-Q1 2026 earnings despite 34% revenue growth: company cut 1,100 employees citing AI replacing internal functions (a self-inflicted moat erosion signal) while also competing in AI edge compute — bifurcated outlook makes this a speculative hold; growth is real but AI commoditization pressure on developer tools and CDN pricing is emerging.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Bear thesis partially validated: -24% market reaction to beat-and-raise signals market sees structural moat erosion; 126x forward P/E has no margin for error

catalysts_general: DOJ criminal + civil investigation confirmed; FY2026 revenue guided down ~2% YoY first time in decade; CEO Hemsley return provides management quality but stock recovered 47% from lows already (Tier 1 — TheStreet; NBC News)

Discovery: Down 54% from $404 peak, trading at multi-year low 15.5x P/E with 3.2% dividend yield — 10 directors bought on April 1 while Q1 2026 EPS beat by 10%; DOJ Medicare investigation is the hangover but repricing is 90% complete.
growth_picks.analyst_take: DOJ criminal + civil investigation creates 1-3 year overhang; revenue declining 2% YoY first time in decade; recovery from $270 absorbed most easy money

catalysts_general: CEO Fernandez bought 28,050 shares at $8.90 May 11; FY2027 guide: organic revenue -3-5%, EPS $2.40-2.90; BMO cut PT from $17 to $10; 22% short interest (Tier 1 — DXC PR Newswire)

Discovery: CEO Raul J. Fernandez bought ~28,050 shares for ~$250K at $8.90 on May 11 2026, an open-market purchase at multi-year lows for this IT services turnaround; stock has fallen sharply and CEO buying sub-$9 with personal funds suggests the turnaround thesis is intact; bullish options sentiment (P/C OI ratio 0.86).
growth_picks.analyst_take: CEO buying at multi-year lows; $600M+ FCF at $1.3B market cap extreme value IF revenue stabilizes; FY2027 guide confirms revenue still declining 3-5%

avoid_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Bookings growth decelerating from 20% to 6% guided for Q2 2026; CEO issued stark forecast; AI tutor competition from LLMs structurally displacing model; analyst target $100 is BELOW current price (Tier 1 — company guidance; Tier 4 — TheStreet)

Discovery: Stock down ~42% YTD 2026 as AI tutors (ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini) commoditize language learning and eliminate the need for gamified repetition — the core Duolingo moat; switching costs near zero as AI tutors offer personalized, conversational practice at comparable or lower cost, threatening DAU and subscription retention.
avoid_picks.analyst_take: Bear thesis confirmed: bookings deceleration to 6% growth guided for Q2 2026; AI tutors structurally displacing gamified model; 21.6% SI already positioned short
